message:
chore(beads): sync issue status updates

- Documented blocked status on aii.4 (galaxy-brain tests)
- Cross-surface consistency tests blocked by 8gfb, wme
- All 23 ledger math tests passing
- All 3 redaction safety tests passing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -152,7 +152,7 @@
 {"id":"process_triage-aii.1","title":"Implement E2E action-tray tests (pause/throttle/renice/cgroups) with detailed logging","description":"## Overview
Add end-to-end tests specifically for the **expanded action space** (Plan §6) and the **staged execution protocol** (observe/mitigate before kill), with strong, structured logging to diagnose failures.

These tests complement the general CLI E2E bead by focusing on the highest-risk integration surface: action planning + policy gating + execution + verification.

## Scenarios (must be covered)
### 1) Pause → observe → resume
- Spawn a parent+child workload
- Apply pause to PGID
- Verify CPU drops and process remains present
- Resume and verify recovery

### 2) Throttle (cgroup CPU) → verify relief
- In an isolated test cgroup, throttle CPU
- Verify CPU usage drops (within tolerance)
- Restore previous settings

### 3) Renice → verify scheduling change
- Renice a CPU burner
- Verify nice value changes and is logged

### 4) Kill staged escalation
- SIGTERM with grace period
- If still alive, SIGKILL
- Verify termination and no PID-reuse mixups

### 5) Safety gates (robot mode)
- Ensure robot mode refuses actions when:
  - protected patterns match
  - data-loss gate triggers
  - identity revalidation fails
  - blast-radius caps exceeded

## Logging requirements
- Capture:
  - the generated plan (JSON)
  - every action attempt (structured)
  - verification results
  - failure recovery hints
- On failure, print a minimal replay bundle reference (session artifact dir) to aid debugging.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Tests cover the scenarios above on supported CI environments (or skip with explicit capability checks).
- [ ] Failures produce actionable logs (plan + action outcomes + verification).
- [ ] Tests are deterministic (bounded timeouts, controlled fixtures).

## Test Plan
- E2E: run in CI with capability detection (skip cgroup tests when unsupported).
- Local: provide a script to run these tests with verbose logging.

 {"id":"process_triage-aii.2","title":"E2E: .ptb bundle export/import integrity tests (profiles, checksums, redaction)","description":"## Purpose
Add end-to-end tests that prove `.ptb` bundles are:
- portable
- integrity-checked (manifest + checksums)
- redaction-safe
- reproducible (importing the bundle recreates the same session summary/report surface)

These tests are intentionally **black-box** at the CLI boundary: they validate what users/agents actually rely on.

## Plan requirements covered
From Plan §3.6 + §11:
- `.ptb` is a single-file export container (ZIP default) with:
  - `manifest.json` (schema versions, tool versions/capabilities, host fingerprint, redaction policy version, checksums)
  - `plan.json`
  - telemetry partitions (profile-dependent)
  - optional `raw/` (capped + redacted)
  - optional `report.html` (single file)
- Export profiles:
  - `minimal` (plan + summary only)
  - `safe` (derived features/inference; aggressive redaction)
  - `forensic` (more raw; still policy-redacted; optional encryption handled separately)
- Import must validate checksums and **fail closed** on mismatch.

## Test scope
### 1) Round-trip correctness by profile
For each profile (`minimal`, `safe`, `forensic`):
- Generate a fixture session.
- `pt agent export --profile <profile> --out fixture.ptb`.
- Re-import/read the bundle (via the bundle reader or `pt agent show/report` path).
- Assert:
  - `manifest.json` exists and includes required version fields.
  - `plan.json` exists and matches the original plan’s stable fields (session_id, candidate identities, recommended actions).
  - The report surface renders (either embedded report.html or generated from bundle contents) without missing required fields.

### 2) Integrity failure (tamper tests)
- Flip a byte in the bundle.
- Assert import fails with a deterministic error code and an explicit “checksum mismatch” classification.

### 3) Redaction safety regression tests
Using fixtures containing known sensitive strings:
- Ensure secrets/PII **do not appear** in:
  - `plan.json`
  - `manifest.json`
  - `report.html` (if included)
  - any embedded raw artifacts for `safe`

### 4) Size/overhead sanity
- Large fixture (500–1000 synthetic processes) exports within reasonable size bounds.
- Export time is logged and bounded (regression alert when it grows).

## Fixtures
- A small deterministic fixture with a mix of:
  - orphan-with-supervision
  - zombie remediation case
  - a high-blast-radius server process (to ensure the bundle contains risk metadata)
- A large deterministic fixture for performance.

## Logging requirements (tests)
On failure, tests must log:
- profile
- bundle size
- checksum list and which checksum failed
- redaction scan hits (matched patterns)
- import error classification and stderr

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Round-trip export/import works for `minimal`, `safe`, and `forensic` profiles.
- [ ] Tampered bundles fail closed with clear error classification.
- [ ] Redaction invariants hold across bundle contents.
- [ ] Size/time metrics are recorded for regression tracking.

## Test Plan
- E2E: CLI round-trips and tamper tests.
- Property-ish: generate multiple fixtures with randomized sensitive strings and assert redaction.

 {"id":"process_triage-aii.3","title":"E2E: Dormant daemon (ptd) tests (overhead, triggers, cooldown, inbox)","description":"## Purpose
Add end-to-end tests for **dormant mode** (always-on guardian) so we can trust:
- it stays low-overhead,
- it triggers when it should,
- it does not spam/loop,
- it produces the correct artifacts (session + inbox entry), and
- it never violates safety defaults.

This is explicitly required by Plan §11 (“Dormant daemon tests: low overhead, trigger correctness, cooldown/backoff behavior, escalation produces a session + inbox entry”).

## Background (Plan §3.7 + §10 Phase 8/daemon-related + §11)
Dormant mode is special because it runs continuously; a bug here can degrade the user’s machine or flood them with noise. Tests must therefore validate both correctness **and** resource/operational behavior.

## Scope
### What we are testing
1. **Overhead budget**
   - CPU and memory usage of daemon loop under idle conditions.
   - No unbounded log/event growth.

2. **Trigger correctness**
   - When sustained evidence thresholds are met, daemon escalates to create a triage session.
   - Trigger windows are robust to transient spikes (EWMA + sustained windows).

3. **Cooldown / backoff**
   - After a trigger, cooldown prevents repeated sessions for the same condition.
   - Failure paths use backoff (no tight crash loops).

4. **Artifact + inbox correctness**
   - A trigger produces:
     - durable `session_id`
     - artifact directory (plan + samples + derived + telemetry pointers)
     - inbox item that is actionable by humans/agents

5. **Coordination invariants**
   - Respects per-user `pt lock` (does not race manual runs / agent runs).
   - Does not compete for heavy probes when lock is held (queues inbox instead).

6. **Safety invariants**
   - Dormant mode is advisory-only by default (no auto-kill).
   - Any “suggested” actions are gated by policy and do not execute.

## Test Harness Requirements
- Provide a **test mode** for daemon execution:
  - controllable time source (fake clock) to test cooldown/backoff deterministically
  - deterministic fixture inputs for trigger conditions
  - explicit “single tick” execution for unit-style control

- Provide process fixtures:
  - synthetic process set generator OR controlled lightweight fixture processes
  - ability to simulate signals (CPU deltas, memory growth, orphaning) without requiring privileged ops

## Concrete Test Cases (minimum)
- `test_ptd_idle_low_overhead`
  - Run daemon in idle mode for N ticks; assert CPU/mem within budget; assert no unbounded file growth.

- `test_ptd_trigger_creates_session_and_inbox`
  - Feed sustained trigger evidence; assert session artifacts created; inbox item produced with correct session_id.

- `test_ptd_cooldown_prevents_repeat_sessions`
  - Trigger once; continue feeding evidence; verify no second session until cooldown expires.

- `test_ptd_backoff_on_failure`
  - Force an internal failure (simulated tool timeout / write error); assert backoff increases; no tight loop.

- `test_ptd_respects_pt_lock`
  - Hold the lock (simulate active manual run); feed trigger evidence; daemon must queue inbox item (or defer) without performing heavy work.

- `test_ptd_escalation_ladder_notifications`
  - Validate notification/escalation ladder behavior (quiet → notify → high urgency), including dedupe.

## Logging Requirements (tests)
- Every test prints a structured “why” block on failure:
  - trigger threshold inputs, computed EWMA/window state
  - cooldown/backoff counters
  - session_id + artifact paths
  - inbox payload summary
  - measured overhead metrics (CPU %, RSS, IO bytes)

- Capture daemon logs and assert presence of:
  - `ptd.tick.start/end`
  - `ptd.trigger.evaluated`
  - `ptd.trigger.fired`
  - `ptd.cooldown.active`
  - `ptd.inbox.enqueued`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tests above exist and pass reliably.
- [ ] Tests are deterministic (fake clock / fixed fixtures) and do not require root.
- [ ] Logs on failure are sufficient to diagnose “why did it trigger?” or “why didn’t it trigger?”

## Dependencies
- Dormant daemon epic: `process_triage-b4v`
- Daemon core loop: `process_triage-nh7p`
- Inbox command surface: `process_triage-iqe`
- Session model/artifacts: `process_triage-qje`
- Escalation/notifications: `process_triage-a3h0`, `process_triage-1k6`

-{"id":"process_triage-aii.4","title":"Tests: Galaxy-brain math ledger consistency (TUI/agent/report)","description":"## Purpose
Add tests that guarantee the **galaxy‑brain math ledger** is:
- internally consistent (equations ↔ substituted numbers ↔ computed outputs),
- consistent across surfaces (TUI, `pt agent explain`, HTML report), and
- safe to share (redaction applied; no secrets leak).

This is explicitly required by Plan §11 (“Galaxy-brain mode tests: math ledger includes equations + concrete numbers and matches the underlying inference outputs.”).

## Background (Plan §7.8 + §11)
Galaxy‑brain mode is a trust feature: it must be correct, reproducible, and auditable. A mismatch between displayed math and actual decisions destroys trust.

## Scope
### 1) Ledger content correctness
For a deterministic fixture session, assert ledger includes (at minimum):
- Posterior by class `P(C|x)` and log-odds
- Bayes factors / evidence strengths for top features
- Expected-loss table and chosen action
- FDR/e‑FDR selection summary (k chosen, α used, membership)
- Alpha‑investing wealth state (if enabled)
- VOI computations for next best probe (if enabled)

### 2) Substituted-number correctness
- Each “math card” includes:
  - equation template
  - substituted numeric values
  - resulting numeric output
- Test asserts substituted numbers match the actual numeric fields used in computation.

### 3) Cross-surface consistency
Given the same session fixture:
- `pt agent explain --galaxy-brain` output matches the ledger stored in session artifacts.
- HTML report “Galaxy‑Brain” tab matches the same ledger data (modulo formatting).
- TUI drilldown (if snapshot-testable) presents the same key values.

### 4) Redaction safety
- Ledger never contains raw sensitive strings.
- Any command lines / paths / env var evidence shown in galaxy-brain mode must use redacted/hashes.

## Suggested Test Strategy
- Unit tests:
  - validate ledger JSON schema and required fields
  - validate card-by-card numeric equality within tolerances

- Golden tests:
  - fixture session inputs → exact expected ledger outputs (stable formatting)

- Snapshot tests:
  - report HTML contains a Galaxy‑Brain tab and embeds the ledger (or references embedded JSON) correctly

## Concrete Test Cases (minimum)
- `test_galaxy_brain_ledger_schema_and_required_cards`
- `test_galaxy_brain_posterior_numbers_match_inference`
- `test_galaxy_brain_expected_loss_numbers_match_decision`
- `test_galaxy_brain_fdr_selection_matches_decision_output`
- `test_galaxy_brain_report_tab_renders_and_contains_ledger`
- `test_galaxy_brain_redaction_no_secrets_in_ledger`

## Logging Requirements (tests)
- On failure, print:
  - the ledger card ID
  - expected numeric values and actual numeric values
  - the source-of-truth struct/field name used to compute the value
  - a compact diff of ledger JSON

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ledger math tests pass deterministically on fixtures.
- [ ] Cross-surface consistency is verified (agent + report at minimum).
- [ ] Redaction invariants are verified by scanning outputs.

## Dependencies
- Evidence ledger generation: `process_triage-myq`
- Posterior computation: `process_triage-e48`
- Bayes factors: `process_triage-0ij`
- Decision/expected loss: `process_triage-d88`
- FDR selection: `process_triage-sqe`
- Alpha-investing: `process_triage-cpm`
- VOI: `process_triage-brh7`
- Agent explain surface: `process_triage-e3qe`
- Galaxy-brain UI display: `process_triage-wme`, `process_triage-8gfb`
- HTML report generator (galaxy-brain tab): `process_triage-k4yc.5`

,{"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.4","depends_on_id":"process_triage-8gfb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:30:04.420858608-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.4","depends_on_id":"process_triage-k4yc.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:30:04.648838723-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}],"comments":[{"id":73,"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 6 new tests to galaxy_brain_consistency.rs:

1. test_galaxy_brain_expected_loss_numbers_match_decision - Verifies expected loss computation matches decision outcome, including manual verification of Kill action loss formula
2. test_galaxy_brain_expected_loss_useful_process - Verifies expected loss hierarchy for useful-classified processes (Keep < Kill)
3. test_galaxy_brain_fdr_selection_matches_decision_output - Verifies FDR selection results (e-values, p-values, selection thresholds, sorting)
4. test_galaxy_brain_fdr_monotonicity - Verifies FDR monotonicity property (higher alpha -> more selections)
5. test_galaxy_brain_ledger_to_json_consistency - Verifies EvidenceLedger JSON serialization roundtrip
6. test_galaxy_brain_multiple_scenarios_consistency - Tests consistency across abandoned_orphan, useful_active, and uncertain_mixed scenarios

All 20 tests pass. This addresses acceptance criteria for ledger math tests passing deterministically on fixtures.

Note: HTML report tab tests (test_galaxy_brain_report_tab_renders_and_contains_ledger) deferred pending galaxy-brain mode implementation (process_triage-8gfb, process_triage-wme).","created_at":"2026-01-17T05:18:38Z"},{"id":74,"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 3 redaction safety tests to galaxy_brain_consistency.rs:

1. test_galaxy_brain_ledger_no_secrets_leak - Verifies EvidenceLedger JSON output doesn't contain sensitive patterns (AWS keys, GitHub tokens, JWTs, API keys, passwords, etc.)
2. test_galaxy_brain_data_no_secrets_leak - Verifies GalaxyBrainData JSON output is clean
3. test_galaxy_brain_why_summary_no_secrets - Verifies why_summary and top_evidence fields don't expose secrets

All 23 tests pass. This completes the 'Redaction invariants are verified by scanning outputs' acceptance criterion.

Total tests in galaxy_brain_consistency.rs now: 23
- 14 original tests
- 6 expected loss/FDR tests (previous session)
- 3 redaction safety tests (this session)","created_at":"2026-01-17T05:32:04Z"}]}

### 3) Cross-surface consistency
Given the same session fixture:

- TUI drilldown (if snapshot-testable) presents the same key values.

### 4) Redaction safety

- Unit tests:
  - validate ledger JSON schema and required fields

  - report HTML contains a Galaxy‑Brain tab and embeds the ledger (or references embedded JSON) correctly

## Concrete Test Cases (minimum)
- `test_galaxy_brain_ledger_schema_and_required_cards`
- `test_galaxy_brain_posterior_numbers_match_inference`
- `test_galaxy_brain_expected_loss_numbers_match_decision`
- `test_galaxy_brain_fdr_selection_matches_decision_output`

- `test_galaxy_brain_redaction_no_secrets_in_ledger`

## Logging Requirements (tests)
- On failure, print:
  - the ledger card ID
  - expected numeric values and actual numeric values
  - the source-of-truth struct/field name used to compute the value
  - a compact diff of ledger JSON

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Ledger math tests pass deterministically on fixtures.
- [ ] Cross-surface consistency is verified (agent + report at minimum).
- [ ] Redaction invariants are verified by scanning outputs.

## Dependencies

6 new tests to galaxy_brain_consistency.rs:

1. test_galaxy_brain_expected_loss_numbers_match_decision - Verifies expected loss computation matches decision outcome, including manual verification of Kill action loss formula
2. test_galaxy_brain_expected_loss_useful_process - Verifies expected loss hierarchy for useful-classified processes (Keep < Kill)
3. test_galaxy_brain_fdr_selection_matches_decision_output - Verifies FDR selection results (e-values, p-values, selection thresholds, sorting)
4. test_galaxy_brain_fdr_monotonicity - Verifies FDR monotonicity property (higher alpha -> more selections)
5. test_galaxy_brain_ledger_to_json_consistency - Verifies EvidenceLedger JSON serialization roundtrip
6. test_galaxy_brain_multiple_scenarios_consistency - Tests consistency across abandoned_orphan, useful_active, and uncertain_mixed scenarios

All 20 tests pass. This addresses acceptance criteria for ledger math tests passing deterministically on fixtures.

Note: HTML report tab tests (test_galaxy_brain_report_tab_renders_and_contains_ledger) deferred pending galaxy-brain mode implementation (process_triage-8gfb, process_triage-wme).","created_at":"2026-01-17T05:18:38Z"},{"id":74,"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Added 3 redaction safety tests to galaxy_brain_consistency.rs:

2. test_galaxy_brain_data_no_secrets_leak - Verifies GalaxyBrainData JSON output is clean
3. test_galaxy_brain_why_summary_no_secrets - Verifies why_summary and top_evidence fields don't expose secrets

All 23 tests pass. This completes the 'Redaction invariants are verified by scanning outputs' acceptance criterion.

Total tests in galaxy_brain_consistency.rs now: 23
- 14 original tests
- 6 expected loss/FDR tests (previous session)
- 3 redaction safety tests (this session)","created_at":"2026-01-17T05:32:04Z"},{"id":75,"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.4","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Cross-surface consistency tests remain blocked:

When 8gfb/wme are implemented, add:

- test_galaxy_brain_tui_drilldown_matches_ledger","created_at":"2026-01-17T05:35:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-aii.5","title":"Regression suite: advanced inference layer sanity tests (BOCPD/Hawkes/EVT/sketches/IMM/PPC/DRO)","description":"## Purpose
Plan §11 lists a large set of **advanced inference-layer regression/sanity tests** (BOCPD, Hawkes, EVT, sketches, periodicity, IMM, PPC, DRO, submodular probe selection, etc.). These are not optional in the plan: they are required to keep the system “alien artifact” level rather than a bag of heuristics.

This bead creates an explicit home for those tests so they don’t get lost as scattered “we’ll test it later” notes inside implementation beads.

## Scope
### What this suite covers (from Plan §11)
- Hawkes / marked point process fit sanity tests
- BOCPD change-point detection regression tests
- FDR-gating tests (many-process safety) beyond unit-level
- EVT tail-fitting regression tests
- Sketch/heavy-hitter tests (accuracy vs resource budget)
- Belief propagation correctness tests on PPID trees
- Periodicity feature regression tests
- IMM filter regression tests
- Online FDR / alpha-investing tests
- Posterior predictive check tests (detect misspecification)
- DRO gating tests (conservative under drift)
- Submodular probe selection tests (monotonicity/approx sanity)

## Design Requirements
- Tests must be deterministic:
  - fixed RNG seeds
  - fixture datasets checked into test resources
  - bounded runtime

- Tests must validate both:
  - **numerical sanity** (no NaNs, stable outputs, plausible magnitudes)
  - **behavioral sanity** (expected directionality: drift ↑ → gates tighten; change-point triggers on regime shift; etc.)

- Tests must be usable as debugging tools:
  - emit detailed logs describing which invariant failed and why

## Concrete Test Categories
### 1) BOCPD regression
- Fixture sequences with known change points.
- Assert run-length posterior concentrates near true τ.

### 2) Hawkes / marked point process sanity
- Simulated Hawkes process fixtures (with known excitation params).
- Assert fitted/estimated excitation is within tolerance.
- Validate that summaries feed the core as deterministic features (no hidden iterative decision dependence).

### 3) EVT tail modeling regression
- Synthetic heavy-tail exceedances with known GPD parameters.
- Assert parameter estimates and exceedance probabilities are within tolerance.

### 4) Sketch/heavy-hitter accuracy
- Synthetic high-rate event streams.
- Validate Count-Min/Space-Saving error bounds empirically.
- Validate memory/time budgets.

### 5) Belief propagation correctness
- Small PPID trees with manually computed marginals.
- Assert sum-product matches exact enumeration.

### 6) Periodicity + IMM regression
- Synthetic periodic/noisy signals.
- Validate periodicity features recover the dominant period.
- Validate IMM regime tracking behaves sensibly on regime-switch fixtures.

### 7) PPC + DRO gating
- Fixtures where model is well-specified vs misspecified.
- Assert PPC flags misspecification and that DRO gate tightens action thresholds.

### 8) Online FDR/alpha-investing sanity
- Simulated streams of hypotheses with known null/alt mix.
- Assert wealth never goes negative; selection respects budget.

### 9) Submodular probe selection
- Synthetic probe overlap graphs.
- Assert monotonicity and diminishing returns; greedy achieves expected near-optimal.

## Logging Requirements (tests)
- Each test prints:
  - fixture ID / seed
  - key intermediate metrics (e.g., τ posterior mass, fitted params, error bounds)
  - expected vs actual values
  - performance metrics (time, memory) where relevant

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Suite exists with the categories above (no silent omissions).
- [ ] Tests run in CI within an agreed time budget.
- [ ] Failures are diagnosable from logs alone.

## Dependencies
This suite is intentionally blocked on the relevant feature implementations:
- BOCPD: `process_triage-lfrb`
- Hawkes: `process_triage-hxh`
- Marked point process summaries: `process_triage-cfon.8`
- EVT: `process_triage-fh0d`
- Sketches: `process_triage-nao.5`
- Belief propagation: `process_triage-d7s`
- Periodicity: `process_triage-nao.2`
- IMM: `process_triage-nao.6`
- Alpha-investing: `process_triage-cpm`
- PPC: `process_triage-0uy`
- DRO: `process_triage-6a1`
- Submodular probe selection: `process_triage-p15.3`

comprehensive advanced_inference_regression.rs test file with 34 passing tests covering BOCPD, Hawkes/MPP, EVT, Sketches, Belief Propagation, IMM, PPC, DRO, Alpha Investing, and Submodular probe rocess_triage-6a1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:31:29.378760493-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.5","depends_on_id":"process_triage-p15.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T07:31:29.606442727-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-aii.6","title":"EPIC: Non-mocked coverage + real-system E2E logging","description":"## Context
We do not have full unit coverage without mocks, and current E2E coverage is partial and mostly smoke-level. We need a deliberate, real-system test track to validate behavior without mocks/fakes where feasible.

## Goals
- Build a no-mocks test track for core logic and collectors using real processes/sockets/files.
- Add end-to-end integration scripts with rich, structured logging and clear artifacts.
- Gate tests by environment capabilities (Linux, /proc, permissions) rather than mocking.

## Non-Goals
- Replacing existing mock-based tests. This epic adds a complementary track.
- Introducing destructive tests; all tests must be safe and self-contained.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A full dependency map of the no-mock test track exists in Beads.
- [ ] Real-system integration tests cover core collectors and action safety paths.
- [ ] E2E scripts produce detailed logs and artifacts for diagnosis.
- [ ] CI supports running the no-mock track with clear gating.

## Notes
- This epic should align with the Testing Infrastructure and Quality epic.
- Each subtask must document environment assumptions and skip logic.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T10:43:02.283830373-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T10:43:02.283830373-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.6","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-01-15T10:46:08.827475774-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"process_triage-aii.6.1","title":"Audit test coverage: mocked vs real-system map","description":"## Objective
Produce a concrete coverage map that explicitly labels which tests are mock-based, fixture-based, or real-system (no mocks), and highlights gaps in critical paths.

## Why
We cannot claim full no-mock coverage without a structured inventory. This audit is the dependency anchor for all subsequent test work.

## Tasks
- Inventory existing Rust unit/integration tests and BATS tests.
- Tag each test group with one of: **mocked**, **fixture-based**, **real-system**.
- Identify untested or mock-only areas in:
  - collectors (/proc, network, cgroup, systemd)
  - decision/inference math
  - CLI workflows (scan/history/clear/robot)
- Produce a gap report and a target list for no-mock additions.

## Deliverables
- A markdown report in `docs/test_coverage_map.md` with the tags and gaps.
- A short summary in the issue comments with top 5 risk gaps.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All existing test suites are classified.
- [ ] Gaps are enumerated with concrete module/function names.
- [ ] Report includes environment constraints for no-mock tests.

## Dependencies
- None (first anchor task).

## Notes
- This task should not add new tests; only inventory and mapping.
- Use `rg` to discover tests and avoid assumptions.
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T10:44:36.990743828-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-15T12:47:04.233694332-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-15T12:47:04.233696426-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.6.1","depends_on_id":"process_triage-aii.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-15T10:44:36.992695684-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}],"comments":[{"id":12,"issue_id":"process_triage-aii.6.1","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Coverage map drafted in docs/test_coverage_map.md. Top gaps (no-mock): (1) live /proc collector validation, (2) live network collector + inode correlation, (3) live cgroup/systemd detection, (4) ToolRunner timeout/kill/truncation against real commands, (5) real-system CLI E2E with structured logging/artifacts.","created_at":"2026-01-15T17:46:55Z"}]}
